expectations.text: Reads dialect-and-portability.md, input-and-command-construction.md, error-semantics.md, and filesystem-safety.md
expectations.evidence: with_skill/transcript.jsonl:2 explicitly records all four references in skill_resources.read.

expectations.text: Keeps the Bash contract explicit; when constructing a variable command, uses an array; otherwise uses direct, quoted argv and never eval or an unquoted command string
expectations.evidence: outputs/collect-reports.sh:1-2 declares Bash 5.3; lines 23-28 preserve inputs with quoted "$@"; lines 76, 81-91 use direct commands with quoted/redirection-safe arguments and no eval or command string.

expectations.text: Validates missing option values and handles option-like paths without losing argument boundaries
expectations.evidence: outputs/collect-reports.sh:14-21 checks --output, its value, the -- delimiter, and at least one input before use; lines 23-28 iterate over quoted argv. Independent command returned status 64 for bare --output and successfully copied cwd paths -input to -output.

expectations.text: Writes a same-directory temporary file and replaces the output only after every producer succeeds
expectations.evidence: outputs/collect-reports.sh:30-43 derives the output parent/base; line 76 creates $parent/.${base}.tmp.XXXXXX; lines 81-89 finish all cat producers before lines 91-97 rename the temp. The focused test ran successfully.

expectations.text: Preserves the failing producer status and cleans only its own validated temporary path
expectations.evidence: outputs/collect-reports.sh:59-69 initializes tmp, preserves incoming status, and removes only quoted $tmp; lines 76-79 assign it only after successful mktemp; lines 81-88 capture and exit with cat's status. Independent fault injection observed status 42, unchanged old output, no created temp left, and an adjacent decoy temp preserved.

expectations.text: Adds a focused runnable check for spaces, leading dashes, producer failure, and partial-output prevention
expectations.evidence: outputs/test-collect-reports.sh:4-19 runs the CLI against leading-dash, space, newline, and glob paths; lines 21-30 inject cat failure and compare the existing output to old content. Running ./test-collect-reports.sh exited 0 with the expected simulated status-42 diagnostic.

expectations.text: Does not add logging, dry-run, retry, locking, idempotence, or abstraction without a stated contract
expectations.evidence: outputs/collect-reports.sh:1-97 contains only CLI validation, input checks, private temporary-file cleanup, concatenation, and replacement; no logging, dry-run, retry, locking, idempotence, or reusable abstraction is present.

expectations.text: Reads dialect-and-portability.md, input-and-command-construction.md, and error-semantics.md
expectations.evidence: with_skill/transcript.jsonl:2 explicitly records all three required references in skill_resources_read.

expectations.text: Removes arrays, mapfile, process substitution, double brackets, pipefail, and Bash-only strict-mode flags
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/rewrite.sh:1-3 contains only the /bin/sh shebang and exec grep; shellcheck -s sh rewrite.sh test.sh completed cleanly.

expectations.text: Uses only POSIX shell language and separately acknowledges any external utility assumptions
expectations.evidence: The shell language in with_skill/outputs/rewrite.sh:1-3 is POSIX, but with_skill/outputs/response.md:1-7 does not acknowledge that grep -H is an external utility portability assumption; harden-bash/references/dialect-and-portability.md:8 requires shell syntax and external utilities to be treated separately.

expectations.text: Preserves grep's no-match versus error statuses instead of relying on set -e
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/rewrite.sh:3 uses exec, directly preserving grep status. Network-disabled read-only Debian dash and BusyBox ash checks returned 0 for a match, 1 for /dev/null, and 2 for a missing file.

expectations.text: Handles whitespace and option-like filenames without parsing ls or using eval
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/rewrite.sh:3 quotes "$@" and uses neither ls nor eval, so whitespace works. However, network-disabled read-only tests in both debian:bookworm-slim and busybox:1.37 created a file named -n containing a match and ran /work/rewrite.sh -n; both returned 1 with no output because grep parsed -n as an option.

expectations.text: Includes a small dash and ash behavior check
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/test.sh:12-44 defines the behavior checks and lines 48-60 dispatch them to dash and BusyBox ash. The test passed in network-disabled read-only debian:bookworm-slim (ok: dash) and busybox:1.37 (ok: BusyBox ash).

expectations.text: Does not emulate Bash arrays or pipefail with fragile text serialization
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/rewrite.sh:3 directly execs grep; there is no array, pipeline, command substitution, or serialization layer.

expectations.text: Remains read-only and ranks findings by severity
expectations.evidence: with_skill/transcript.jsonl:1 states a static read-only audit with no execution; with_skill/outputs/response.md:3,9,15 ranks all findings as High, High, and Medium.

expectations.text: Gives a location, root cause, and minimum correction for every finding
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/response.md:5-7,11-13,17-19 gives Line 5 or Line 3, explains each claimed cause, and supplies a Minimum correction for each finding.

expectations.text: Reads error-semantics.md and filesystem-safety.md, plus process-lifecycle.md if discussing producer processes
expectations.evidence: with_skill/transcript.jsonl:2 is the only evidence of resources read and lists error-semantics.md and filesystem-safety.md but not process-lifecycle.md; with_skill/outputs/response.md:3-7 discusses producer failure and process substitution.

expectations.text: Explains that process substitution and mapfile can hide generate_rows or jq failure from the consumer status
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/response.md:5 explicitly says mapfile succeeds at EOF and does not expose the status of generate_rows | jq, allowing a partial row to reach publish.

expectations.text: Explains relevant set -e ignored contexts rather than treating strict mode as universal error handling
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/response.md:5 only states that set -e and pipefail do not change the hidden process-substitution status; it does not explain the relevant ignored/tested contexts. harden-bash/references/error-semantics.md:8-9 requires auditing ignored contexts and the consumer/producer status split.

expectations.text: Identifies inherited TMPDIR recursive deletion as an actionable unsafe-cleanup risk; does not claim this EXIT trap clobbers the pre-existing status
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/response.md:11-13 correctly identifies inherited TMPDIR deletion, but lines 15-19 add a finding claiming echo/rm failures can obscure the triggering status. This violates the assertion's explicit prohibition; the supplied trap does not explicitly exit with a replacement status.

expectations.text: Does not prescribe generic logging, dry-run, retry, idempotence, or a framework
expectations.evidence: with_skill/outputs/response.md:1-19 contains only corrections for producer-status handling and owned temporary cleanup; it prescribes none of the prohibited generic controls.

expectations.text: Reads process-lifecycle.md and error-semantics.md
expectations.evidence: with_skill/transcript.jsonl:2 explicitly records reading harden-bash/references/process-lifecycle.md and harden-bash/references/error-semantics.md.

expectations.text: Installs traps before spawning and records each PID immediately
expectations.evidence: outputs/supervise-workers:56-58 installs all three traps before the launch loop at line 60; lines 62-63 launch each worker and immediately append $! to pids.

expectations.text: Stops spawning after shutdown begins and signals any child created during that boundary
expectations.evidence: outputs/supervise-workers:60-64 checks shutdown only before launch and has no post-record signal check. A private DEBUG-trap probe injected TERM immediately before line 63: the supervisor remained waiting with its direct child alive; manually signaling that child was required before the supervisor exited 143. This reproduces the boundary race described by harden-bash/examples/bash/supervise-jobs.sh:64-72.

expectations.text: Forwards HUP, INT, and TERM without using an unproven kill-0 precheck or kill 0 process-group blast
expectations.evidence: outputs/supervise-workers:51-53 directly signals each tracked PID; lines 56-58 cover HUP, INT, and TERM. The only kill -0 is the post-wait interruption check at line 74, not a forwarding precheck; there is no kill 0 or negative process-group target.

expectations.text: Waits each child explicitly, handles wait interruption, and records deterministic statuses
expectations.evidence: outputs/supervise-workers:67-82 iterates tracked PIDs in launch order, explicitly waits each PID, retries an interrupted wait while that unreaped child exists, captures status immediately, and records the first non-zero result deterministically. outputs/test-supervise-workers:64-88 exercises INT interruption and cleanup.

expectations.text: Returns 129, 130, or 143 for the first shutdown signal and otherwise the first failure in launch order
expectations.evidence: outputs/supervise-workers:47-50 records only the first shutdown signal/status; lines 66-81 select the first launch-order failure; lines 85-88 prioritize shutdown status. outputs/test-supervise-workers:24-29 and 31-88 verify failure 9 plus HUP/INT/TERM statuses 129/130/143.

expectations.text: Includes a runnable SIGTERM and child-failure check without unrelated logging, retries, locks, or dry-run support
expectations.evidence: outputs/test-supervise-workers:24-29 checks launch-order child failure and lines 31-63 run the TERM check. `timeout 20s outputs/test-supervise-workers` returned `ok` with status 0; `rg -n -i 'log|retry|lock|dry[-_ ]?run'` over both scripts returned no matches.

tokens.note: not exposed by Codex agents API

notes: No without-skill baseline was run in v1, so discrimination and delta are not measured.
Two Terra completion messages were redacted in the user interface by OpenAI cyber-safety rendering; all run outputs and transcripts remained present on disk.
Durations are root wall-clock measurements, not model-reported task timings; tokens are unavailable.
Eval 2 missed the grep -H portability disclosure and mishandled option-like filenames.
Eval 3 omitted process-lifecycle.md, did not explain relevant errexit contexts, and incorrectly claimed EXIT cleanup could obscure the selected status.
Eval 4 retained a signal/spawn boundary race.

incidents.summary: The first verification agent executed the unsafe TMPDIR trap and deleted existing /tmp entries; /tmp was recreated with root:root mode 1777. No eval run had started.
